description: FIFTH PvP anchor (2025-07-19 22:47:59, #214 X:597 Y:597). [RFJ]Marty rally DEFEAT vs [PBT]FxCat garrison (+12 supporters). First ATTACKER-LOSES anchor and first real battle with DUPLICATE JOINERS (4x Nora). Near-mirror totals: 1,421,184 vs 1,421,819; attacker WIPED, defender kept 261,433 (18.4%). COMP CORRECTED 2026-07-08 (Martin fixed a class mistranslation; first ingest wrongly had 676k LANCERS): attacker = inf 744,843 T11fc9 + MARKSMAN 676,341 T11fc10, NO lancers - both classes on strong ~+2647% attack panels. ROLE NOTE: the revised scenario JSON had roles flipped (own=garrison); report evidence (Attacker panel all [RFJ]; Rally widgets on Marty's side) fixes Marty as the rally ATTACKER. Input: Scenarios/Marty_Tygax_4Nora_Gen10_202507.json (1000 runs seed 4471).

defender.player: [PBT]FxCat garrison + 12 supporters (Milkyo, D Luffy, GaDzIK, HandsomeBoy, Duhmanda, AnRChIp, SelRuhlg, FxQat, Imags, Natty, fuqn chrust, JTT)

specials_note: Both sides: +20% troops A/D/L/H items, -20% enemy A/D items. NO pet buffs (older battle, 2025-07). Attacker widgets: Rally Attack +15%, Rally Lethality +15%. Defender widgets: Defender Defense +15%, Defender Health +15%. All in panel_pct.
skill_kill_oracle: Per-skill kills: att Gregory 17 triggers 64,253 kills (+rows 1/1/42); att Mia 27 / 23 triggers 32,814 kills / 17; att Blanchette 1 / 8x29,491 / 12x95,815 / 3 / 3x11,967. Def Magnus 1/7/1/37 (0 kills); def Freya 1 / 15 triggers 96,582 kills / 1 / 7 / 6x24,293 / 3; def Blanchette 9x22,553 / 13x41,484 / 3 / 12x47,434. Total skill-attributed: att ~234k of 1.16M dealt (~20%), def ~232k of 1.42M dealt (~16%) - HIGHER share than A4's ~7% (older-gen heroes, longer battle).
structural_ground_truth: (1) FIRST ATTACKER DEFEAT: near-mirror totals, defender won keeping 18.4% - pins the far side of the knife-edge and proves near-even rally-vs-garrison defenders fight at >= parity (the A1/A2 inference, now with a defender-win witness). (2) DUPLICATE JOINERS REAL DATA: with the CORRECTED comp this battle genuinely constrains duplicate stacking (see item 4); the earlier 'joiners are not the cause' finding applied to the mistranslated comp only. (3) CORRECTED COMP: attacker = inf 744,843 + marksman 676,341, NO lancers (both classes on strong ~+2647% panels). (4) FINAL STATE (flat lock def_k=0.45; the third-pass size-dependent scale was REVERTED as error-induced). The engine ranks the ATTACKER ahead regardless of joiners (0-joiner p_win 1.00, ~65% survivors; 4x-Nora-dedup ~68%) vs the real DEFEAT. The marksman correction made the attacker look STRONGER than the mistranslated lancers would (marksman +2647% panel vs lancer +1719%), so it WIDENED the sim/reality gap. The miss is therefore the BASE matchup, not Nora: a near-mirror battle (counts within 0.04%, panels within ~15-25%) the engine can't call - flagged coin_flip by the +-20% band. Duplicate-joiner DEDUP is implemented (same-hero joiner SK1 applies once; enemy Jessie x2 dedups symmetrically) as the more-correct mechanic, but it is NOT the driver of this defeat. ERROR-INDEPENDENT FINDING worth a clean future re-calibration: anchors 1/2/5 (all real rallies) suggest large multi-player garrison defenders are STRONGER than flat def_k=0.45 implies (A1/A2 deep 3-6% grinds, A5 defender win) - exactly what the reverted size-dependent scale was targeting; it should be re-derived on corrected data if pursued, NOT resurrected from the mistranslated cliff.
prediction_failure_2026_07_08: FINAL after Martin's class correction + engine revert. On the flat lock the engine predicts a confident attacker WIN (p_win 1.00, ~65% survivors) vs the real DEFEAT - a near-mirror coin-flip the engine ranks the wrong way; flagged coin_flip (not gated in regression #12). The third-pass 'defender needs x1.35-1.45 / size-dependent def_k' analysis was computed from the MISTRANSLATED comp (676k treated as lancers) and is WITHDRAWN; the size-dependent scale it produced was REVERTED to the flat anchor-4 lock. Duplicate-joiner DEDUP was kept (correct mechanic, one-battle evidence) but does not explain this defeat. Real error-independent signal (see structural_ground_truth item 4): large multi-player garrison defenders appear under-credited by flat def_k=0.45.
